ocr.text: In the context of Schneider Electric’s commitment to enhancing operational efficiency through Lean manufacturing principles,
the following metrics highlight the impact of recent automation investments. A key focus has been on reducing lead time, which
has improved from 7 days to 6.5 days, representing a significant reduction of -5%. This improvement not only reflects our
dedication to efficiency but also aligns with our goal of increasing on-target performance, evidenced by MachineUtilization's
OnTarget metric of C.

Furthermore, the operational gains achieved through these initiatives are underscored by the MachineUtilization's Operational
value of EUR200K, indicating a substantial return on our investments.

Additionally, the InventoryTurnover metric showcases an operational value of EUR1.2M, signifying effective inventory
management. Across various projects, we have also noted a positive trend in Time to Market, currently at 30 days, which is
critical for maintaining competitiveness. The integration of robotics into our workflows has yielded a projected ROI of €1.5M,
demonstrating a strong potential for scaling up our processes, tagged as ScaleUp, while achieving an efficiency increase of
15%. Over an 8-month period, our total projects have been rated as Good, further validating the effectiveness of our Lean
initiatives. This data provides a comprehensive overview of the tangible benefits derived from our strategic investments in
automation and Lean methodologies.
